questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

In a certain memory experiment, subject A is able to memorize words at a rate given by

 

In the same memory experiment, subject B is able to memorize at the rate given by 

 

How many more words does subject B memorize from t = 0 to t = 14 (during the first 14 minutes)?
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. In a certain memory experiment, subject A is able to memorize words at a rate given by (dm/dt) = -0.006(t) with superscript (2) + 0.4t (words per minute). In the same memory experiment, subject B is able to memorize at the rate given by (dM/dt) = -0.009(t) with superscript (2) + 0.4t (words per minute). How many more words does subject B memorize from t = 0 to t = 14 (during the first 14 minutes)?

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

The disk (x - 5)2 + y2 ≤ 1 is revolved about the y-axis to generate a torus. Find its volume. (Hint: = π, since it is the area of a semicircle of radius 1.)

questions.prompt_html:

questions.prompt_html:
Find the area of the region bounded by the graph of y =  and the x-axis from x = -1 to x = 1.
(Shown in figure below)
questions.prompt_text: Find the area of the region bounded by the graph of y = (1/1 + (x) with superscript (2)) and the x-axis from x = -1 to x = 1. (Shown in figure below) Short Description: An illustration shows graph of the function y = start fraction 1 over left parenthesis 1 + x squared right parenthesis end fraction.Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 4.5 to 4.5 in increments of 0.5 and the y axis ranges from negative 1 to 1 in increments of 0.2. The graph is a bell shaped curve. The left end approaches negative x axis beyond x = negative 4 and the right end approaches positive x axis beyond x = 4. The peak of the curve is at (0, 1). The vertical lines are drawn from the curve to x axis at x = 1 and x = negative 1.
questions.solution_html:
From symmetry, the total area A = 2 × area of the region from x = 0 to x = 1.
Therefore A = 2 ) dx = 2  dx = 2  dx
From table of basic integrals, we obtain A = 2  = 2 [tan-1(1) - tan-1(0)] = 2 
Therefore, A =  square units.
questions.solution_text: From symmetry, the total area A = 2 × area of the region from x = 0 to x = 1. Therefore A = 2 integral of (((y) with subscript (upper ) - (y) with subscript ( lower)) from (0) to (1) ) dx = 2 integral of (((1/1 + (x) with superscript (2)) - 0)) from (0) to (1) dx = 2 integral of ((1/1 + (x) with superscript (2))) from (0) to (1) dx From table of basic integrals, we obtain A = 2 ((tan) with superscript (-1)(x)|) superscript (x=1) subscript (x=0) = 2 [tan -1 (1) - tan -1 (0)] = 2 ((π/4)) Therefore, A = (π/2 ) square units.

Short Description: An illustration shows graph of three functions.Long Description: The graph of hyperbolic cosine of x is a concave up curve in the first and second quadrant passing through the point (0, 1). The graph of y = negative hyperbolic sine of x is a curve that is concave up in the second quadrant and concave down in the fourth quadrant. The curve passes through the origin. The graph of natural log of 4 is a vertical line at x = 1.4. The y axis from the origin to (0, 1) is highlighted.

questions.prompt_html: If R is the region enclosed by the graphs of y = f(x) and y = g(x) from x = a to x = b (as shown in the figure below), then the volume V of the solid generated by revolving the region R about the line y = -2 is V = πdx.
questions.prompt_text: If R is the region enclosed by the graphs of y = f(x) and y = g(x) from x = a to x = b (as shown in the figure below), then the volume V of the solid generated by revolving the region R about the line y = -2 is V = π integral of ({((f(x) - 2)) with superscript ( 2) - ((g(x) - 2)) with superscript ( 2)}) from (a) to (b) dx. Short Description: A graph shows two curves.Long Description: The graph of y = f of x is a curve that slopes upward with rises and falls. The graph of y = g of x is a curve below the previous curve. The curve concave up on the left and concave down on the right. The points of these two curves are connected by dashed vertical lines on the left and right. Both the curve are between x = a to x = b. The region between the curves is labeled, R. A dashed horizontal line below the x axis is labeled, axis of rotation y = negative 2.

images.alt: integral of ({((f(x) - 2)) with superscript ( 2) - ((g(x) - 2)) with superscript ( 2)}) from (a) to (b)

Short Description: A graph shows two curves.Long Description: The graph of y = f of x is a curve that slopes upward with rises and falls. The graph of y = g of x is a curve below the previous curve. The curve concave up on the left and concave down on the right. The points of these two curves are connected by dashed vertical lines on the left and right. Both the curve are between x = a to x = b. source.answer_key_text: The volume of the solid generated by revolving the region R about the x-axis is given by:

classification.crosswalk_note: Not mapped to the current AP Calculus AB/BC core in this first-pass crosswalk.
classification.framework: AP Calculus Course and Exam Description, current Unit 1 topic framework

questions.prompt_html:
Let R be the plane region enclosed by the graphs of y = f(x) and y = g(x) from x = a to x = b, where a > 0
(as shown in the figure below). Suppose that f(x) + g(x) = 2x for all x ∈ [a, b].
Show that the volume of the solid generated by revolving the plane region R about the x-axis always has the same volume as the solid generated by revolving the region R about the y-axis. 
all x ∈ [a, b]?
questions.prompt_text: Let R be the plane region enclosed by the graphs of y = f(x) and y = g(x) from x = a to x = b, where a > 0 (as shown in the figure below). Suppose that f(x) + g(x) = 2x for all x ∈ [a, b]. Show that the volume of the solid generated by revolving the plane region R about the x-axis always has the same volume as the solid generated by revolving the region R about the y-axis. all x ∈ [a, b]? Short Description: A graph shows two curves.Long Description: The graph of y = f of x is a curve that slopes upward with rises and falls. The graph of y = g of x is a curve below the previous curve. The curve concave up on the left and concave down on the right. The points of these two curves are connected by dashed vertical lines on the left and right. Both the curve are between x = a and x = b. The region between the curves is labeled, R.
questions.solution_html:
The volume of the solid generated by revolving the region R about the x-axis is given by:
Vx = π- (g(x))2] dx ( Using plane slices)
On the other hand, using cylindrical shells method, the volume of the solid generated by revolving region R about the y - axis is given by:

Vy = 2πdx 
Now f(x) + g(x) = 2x, and hence g(x) = 2x - fx).
Substituting g(x) = 2x - f(x) into Vx and Vy and simplifying, we obtain,
Vx = π- (2x-f(x))2] dx = π] dx ............ (1)
Vy = 2πdx = πdx 
  = πdx ............. (2)
Comparing (1) & (2), we obtain Vx = Vy. Proof is complete!
questions.solution_text: The volume of the solid generated by revolving the region R about the x-axis is given by: V x = π integral of ([((f(x))) with superscript (2)) from (a) to (b) - (g(x)) 2 ] dx ( Using plane slices) On the other hand, using cylindrical shells method, the volume of the solid generated by revolving region R about the y - axis is given by: V y = 2π integral of (x[f(x) - g(x)]) from (a) to (b) dx Now f(x) + g(x) = 2x, and hence g(x) = 2x - fx). Substituting g(x) = 2x - f(x) into V x and V y and simplifying, we obtain, V x = π integral of ([((f(x))) with superscript (2)) from (a) to (b) - (2x-f(x)) 2 ] dx = π integral of ([4xf(x) - 4(x) with superscript (2)) from (a) to (b) ] dx ............ (1) V y = 2π integral of (x[f(x) - (2x- f(x))]) from (a) to (b) dx = π integral of (2x[2f(x) - 2x]) from (a) to (b) dx = π integral of ([4xf(x) - 4(x) with superscript (2)]) from (a) to (b) dx ............. (2) Comparing (1) & (2), we obtain V x = V y . Proof is complete!

feedback_html.solution:
The volume of the solid generated by revolving the region R about the x-axis is given by:
Vx = π- (g(x))2] dx ( Using plane slices)
On the other hand, using cylindrical shells method, the volume of the solid generated by revolving region R about the y - axis is given by:

Vy = 2πdx 
Now f(x) + g(x) = 2x, and hence g(x) = 2x - fx).
Substituting g(x) = 2x - f(x) into Vx and Vy and simplifying, we obtain,
Vx = π- (2x-f(x))2] dx = π] dx ............ (1)
Vy = 2πdx = πdx 
  = πdx ............. (2)
Comparing (1) & (2), we obtain Vx = Vy. Proof is complete!
